feat(surveys): set $survey_last_seen_date server-side on survey shown events (#45622)

## Summary

Moves `$survey_last_seen_date` person property updates from the JS SDK to the server-side (plugin-server), so it works for all SDKs (iOS, Android, etc.), not just posthog-js.

When a `survey shown` event is processed, the preprocessing pipeline automatically adds `$survey_last_seen_date` to `$set`, which then flows through normal person property handling.

## Changes

- Added `createEnrichSurveyPersonPropertiesStep()` preprocessing step that enriches survey events with person properties
- Added `SURVEY_EVENTS` and `SURVEY_PERSON_PROPERTIES` constants for extensibility
- Wired the step into `pre-team-preprocessing-subpipeline.ts`
- Unit tests for the preprocessing step
- E2E tests to verify the behavior end-to-end

## How it works

The step runs in `createPreTeamPreprocessingSubpipeline`:

```
parseHeaders → applyEventRestrictions → parseKafkaMessage → dropExceptionEvents 
→ resolveTeam → validateHistoricalMigration → enrichSurveyPersonProperties
```

When a `survey shown` event passes through, the step adds `$survey_last_seen_date` to `event.properties.$set`. Later, the person processing code reads `$set` and applies it as a normal person property update.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for the preprocessing step
- [x] E2E tests for survey shown events
- [x] CI passes

fix(surveys): reset wizard state on unmount and improve title editing UX (#46067)

## Summary

- **Fix stale state on re-creation**: the survey wizard wasn't resetting form state (name, questions, etc.) when unmounting for new surveys, so creating another survey would carry over the previous survey's data. Now calls `resetSurvey()` on unmount when `id === 'new'`.
- **Inline-editable survey title**: replaced the static template name next to the back button with an `EditableField` rendered as a page-level title, with an accessible `<label>` for screen readers.

fix(ph-ai): fix random thinking message (#45747)

## Problem

When PostHog AI is thinking, the thinking message changes randomly during streaming.

## Changes
Added a `currentThinkingMessage` state to the Max thread logic that:
- Gets set to a random thinking message when starting a new conversation or reconnecting
- Remains stable throughout the streaming process
- Gets reset to null when thread generation completes
- Is used instead of generating a new random message each time the UI renders

feat(recordings): add batch check for session existence and integrate with ViewRecordingButton (#45818)

## Problem

![2026-01-23 16.02.04.gif](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/ee5fe556-1935-44e8-a9f1-5544e77b6173.gif)

When viewing logs or other data with session IDs, users can't easily tell if a recording exists for that session without clicking through to check. This creates friction in the workflow and can lead to wasted time clicking on sessions that don't have recordings.

## Changes

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/dfd1b413-2c98-4561-9e3e-a8e6479e0351.png)
![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/26aad677-5866-441d-9bab-ce67f7a06e17.png)

- Added a new API endpoint `batch_check_exists` to efficiently check if recordings exist for multiple session IDs
- Created a new `sessionRecordingExistsLogic` to manage batched API calls and cache results
- Enhanced `ViewRecordingButton` with a `checkRecordingExists` prop that automatically verifies if a recording exists
- Applied this improvement to session ID links in logs UI components
- Added proper caching for positive results to reduce API load
- Added comprehensive tests for the new functionality

## How did you test this code?

- Added unit tests for the new `batch_check_exists` API endpoint
- Tested caching behavior to ensure positive results are cached while negative results are not
- Verified the UI correctly shows disabled state for non-existent recordings
- Tested with both existing and non-existing session IDs to ensure correct behavior
- Verified that the batching mechanism correctly handles multiple session IDs

feat: Avatar color distinctness (#46032) ## Problem Previously, person avatars with distinct IDs that started with the same character would display identical colors, making it difficult to differentiate between them. This was due to the `PersonIcon` component not passing a stable color index to `ProfilePicture`. ## Changes - **Added `getPersonColorIndex` utility:** A new function in `frontend/src/scenes/persons/person-utils.ts` that generates a stable color index (0-7) by applying a djb2 hash to the person's `distinct_id` (or the first entry in `distinct_ids`). - **Updated `PersonIcon` component:** Modified `frontend/src/scenes/persons/PersonDisplay.tsx` to automatically compute and pass this stable color index to the `ProfilePicture` component. The explicit `index` prop can still override this if provided. - **Added unit tests:** New tests in `frontend/src/scenes/persons/person-utils.test.ts` verify the `getPersonColorIndex` function, including a specific test to ensure distinct IDs starting with the same character produce different color indices. ## How did you test this code? - New unit tests were added for `getPersonColorIndex` and all existing tests were run and passed. - TypeScript checks were run and passed. - Code was formatted. ## Publish to changelog? no --- [Slack Thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C08499A7REU/p1769526140030509?thread_ts=1769526140.030509&cid=C08499A7REU)

fix(logs): handle zero-duration date range in zoom functionality (#46004)

## Problem

When zooming in or out on the logs viewer, the system doesn't handle the edge case where the start and end dates are identical (zero-duration range). This causes unexpected behavior when users try to zoom on a single timestamp.

## Changes

Added handling for zero-duration date ranges in the `zoomDateRange` function by enforcing a minimum range of 1 minute when the start and end dates are identical. This allows users to properly zoom in and out even when viewing a single timestamp.

## How did you test this code?

Added a specific test case that verifies the behavior when given identical start and end timestamps. The test confirms that a 1-minute minimum range is applied and properly expanded when zooming out.

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(surveys): add product intents for conversion funnel tracking (#45959)

## Summary

Adds product intent tracking for surveys to measure the conversion funnel:

**Surveys viewed** → **Add new clicked** → **Survey created** → **Survey launched**

### Changes

- Add `SURVEY_ADD_NEW` intent when clicking "New survey" button
- Add `SURVEY_CREATED` and `SURVEY_LAUNCHED` intents to the survey wizard
- Differentiate wizard-created surveys with `source: survey_wizard` metadata
- Show "Save as draft" button on all wizard steps (not just the final step)

### Why

We need to understand where users drop off in the survey creation flow to identify friction points and improve conversion.

feat(logs): add logsViewerConfigLogic for saved views foundation (#45793)

## Problem

We want to enable saved views for the logs product, but the current filter state is spread across `logsSceneLogic` and tightly coupled to the scene. This makes it difficult to serialize the full view state for saving/loading, and prevents the LogsViewer component from being reusable in other contexts (error tracking, session replay).

This is Phase 1 of the saved views implementation - establishing the data model without changing existing behavior.

## Changes

- Create `LogsViewerFilters` and `LogsViewerConfig` types in `config/types.ts`
- Create `logsViewerConfigLogic` with `setFilter`/`setFilters` actions and `filters` reducer
- Wire all filter components to write to both `logsSceneLogic` (existing) and `logsViewerConfigLogic` (new) in parallel
- Bind `logsViewerConfigLogic` to the LogsViewer component tree via `BindLogic`

The old `logsSceneLogic` still drives the UI - this change only mirrors filter state to the new logic as groundwork for Phase 2 where we'll migrate ownership.

## How did you test this code?

- Added unit tests for `logsViewerConfigLogic` covering:
  - Setting individual filters (parameterized across all filter types)
  - Preserving other filters when setting a single filter
  - Replacing all filters via `setFilters`
  - Keyed instances maintaining separate state
  - Default config values
- TypeScript check passes
- No behavioral change to verify - existing functionality unchanged

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(llm-analytics): use gpt-4.1-nano for temporal summarization workflows (#45870)

## Problem

Batch trace summarization using gpt-4.1-mini is expensive at scale (~$0.40/1M input tokens).

## Changes

- Add `GPT_4_1_NANO` to `OpenAIModel` enum
- Set `gpt-4.1-nano` as default for Temporal summarization workflows
- Update README with model configuration

gpt-4.1-nano is 75% cheaper ($0.10/1M input) with the same 1M token context.

On-demand summarization keeps gpt-4.1-mini as default.

## Does this work well for self-hosted?

N/A - internal LLM analytics feature

- Existing coordinator tests pass

feat(insights): allow goal line color to be changed (#45647)

## Problem

When creating goal lines in an insight, line color is derived automatically from the goal line index and color palette. The user cannot change the color manually.

Border color is already supported in the backend, so only a proper color selection widget is required.

## Changes

- Wrapped SeriesLetter component with a LemonColorPicker which updates the borderColor property of the corresponding GoalLine.
- To keep design clean, kept SeriesLetter component as-is, only with a `cursor-pointer` class.

## How did you test this code?

Manually. Tested all colors in the LemonColorPicker, and custom colors (hex). Checked if the graph line has the right color, and if changes are persisted.

Summary                                                  
                                                           
  Updated chart.js plugins to latest versions and fixed a  
  bogus import bug.                                        
                                                           
  Package Updates                                          
  Package: chartjs-plugin-annotation                       
  Previous: 2.2.1                                          
  New: 3.1.0                                               
  Notes: Module restructure, new features                  
  ────────────────────────────────────────                 
  Package: chartjs-plugin-trendline                        
  Previous: 2.1.2                                          
  New: 3.2.0                                               
  Notes: Exponential regression, fixes                     
  chartjs-plugin-annotation (2.2.1 → 3.1.0)                
                                                           
  Breaking changes:                                        
  - Plugin restructured as ES module                       
                                                           
  New features:                                            
  - hitTolerance option for all annotation types           
  - getAnnotations() function for programmatic access      
  - Integer drawTime option to sync with dataset rendering 
  - Centered label drawing for doughnut controllers        
                                                           
  Bug fixes:                                               
  - Compilation errors when using multiple plugins         
  - Border styling defaults for SKIA canvas                
                                                           
  chartjs-plugin-trendline (2.1.2 → 3.2.0)                 
                                                           
  New features:                                            
  - Exponential regression support                         
  - trendoffset setting for position adjustment            
  - Legend and label support                               
  - Slope percentage calculations                          
                                                           
  Bug fixes:                                               
  - Trendline accuracy and boundary calculations           
  - Module import issues                                   
  - Trendline orientation issues                           
                                                           
  Bug fix                                                  
                                                           
  Removed erroneous import { id } from                     
  'chartjs-plugin-trendline' in HogFunctionFilters.tsx -   
  this was an IDE auto-import mistake that coincidentally  
  worked because the plugin's ID is the string 'trendline'.

---

## Potentially useful new features:                         
  Feature: Exponential regression                          
  Plugin: trendline                                        
  Potential Use: Growth metrics, user acquisition, revenue 
    often follow exponential patterns - linear             
    trendlines can be misleading                           
  ────────────────────────────────────────                 
  Feature: Slope percentage                                
  Plugin: trendline                                        
  Potential Use: Show rate of change directly on trendlines
                                                           
    (e.g., "+12%/week")                                    
  ────────────────────────────────────────                 
  Feature: hitTolerance                                    
  Plugin: annotation                                       
  Potential Use: Easier hover interaction with goal lines  
  ────────────────────────────────────────                 
  Feature: getAnnotations()                                
  Plugin: annotation                                       
  Potential Use: Useful for testing or programmatic access 
  The exponential regression is probably the most valuable 
  - linear trendlines on exponential growth/decay data are 
  mathematically incorrect and can give users wrong        
  impressions about their metrics.

feat(ph-ai): plan mode (#44359)

## Problem
Implement plan mode for the chat agent to allow users to create structured plans before executing tasks. This enhances the agent's ability to handle complex requests by first planning the approach and getting user approval.

## Changes
- Added plan mode support to the chat agent with a dedicated supermode state
- Created plan-specific prompts, executables, and toolkits
- Implemented mode transition from planning to execution
- Added UI support for plan mode in the mode selector
- Extended the conversation serializer to include supermode information
- Created shared plan mode components that can be reused by both chat and research agents

## How did you test this code?
Tests + local testing

## Publish to changelog?
Yes - this adds a new planning capability to PostHog AI that helps users tackle complex tasks more effectively.

chore: Allow `UserProductList` deletion (#45606) ## Problem Admins are unable to delete users or organizations via the Django admin because the associated `UserProductList` objects cannot be cascade-deleted. This is due to the `UserProductListAdmin` explicitly blocking delete permissions. ## Changes Removed the `has_delete_permission` method from `UserProductListAdmin` in `posthog/admin/admins/user_product_list_admin.py`. By default, Django admin allows delete permissions, enabling the cascade deletion of `UserProductList` entries when a user or organization is deleted. ## How did you test this code? Ran existing Django admin tests to ensure no regressions. Manually verified that deleting a user with an associated `UserProductList` now works correctly in the admin. ## Publish to changelog? no --- [Slack Thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C043VJ93L3B/p1769015374518679?thread_ts=1769015374.518679&cid=C043VJ93L3B)

feat: make Rust read configs from Hypercache instead of rebuild (#45253)

* Rust /flags service now reads SDK config from Python's HyperCache instead of rebuilding it from team data.
* Eliminate duplicated config logic between Python's RemoteConfig.build_config() and Rust's config_response_builder.rs.
* Falls back to minimum config on cache miss or invalid format, which should be impossible.
* Session recording quota limiting remains in Rust since it requires per-request checks that Python can't cache.

I want to start compressing some of these lesser-used settings into sub-menus to reduce UI clutter. We need to track user interactions to support this work.

feat(logs): introduce better UX for when no logs are found (#45554)

## Problem

When no logs are found, the current UI simply displays a generic "No logs to display" message without providing users with helpful context or actions. This creates a poor user experience for those trying to troubleshoot missing logs.

## Changes

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/5fdcd460-5601-4b52-9f6e-543e18d6aa83.png)

- Enhanced the empty state UI for logs with a more informative message, documentation link, and visual elements
- Added an "Expand time range" button to help users quickly adjust their search parameters
- Implemented a `zoomDateRange` action to allow users to expand the time range when no logs are found

## How did you test this code?

Tested the empty state UI by:
- Filtering logs with criteria that return no results
- Verifying the new empty state appears with all elements (detective hedgehog, message, documentation link, and expand button)
- Testing the "Expand time range" button functionality to ensure it correctly doubles the date range
- Confirming the UI properly handles the transition from empty state to displaying logs when results are found

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(logs): implement polling for log availability in NoLogsPrompt (#45546)

## Problem

When users set up logs in PostHog, there's currently no visual indication that the system is actively checking for incoming logs. This creates uncertainty for users who have configured their logging client but don't see immediate results.

## Changes

Added a polling mechanism that periodically checks if logs have been received, along with a visual indicator that shows the system is actively watching for logs

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/bc4f9434-bc88-40c2-9442-5ee1070a485e.png)

## How did you test this code?

Tested the component in both states (with and without logs) to ensure the polling mechanism works correctly. Verified that the polling stops once logs are detected, and that the visual indicator displays properly.

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(customers): Show person count in persons list (#45289)

## Problem

A lot of users have asked for the total count of persons in person list, as there is no way to get to that number except with a sql query.

Let's add a generic way to see total/filtered results for DataTable queries and implement it for persons list for now.

## Changes

Added a new count feature to data tables that shows:
- Total count of records in the dataset
- Number of records that match applied filters (when filters are active)

The implementation includes:
- New `showCount` property for DataTableNode
- Added count display component with appropriate entity type labeling (persons, events, groups, sessions)
- Implemented lazy loaders to fetch total and filtered counts without affecting main query performance
- Added logic to detect when filters are active
- Created specialized count queries for different data types

### Count with no filters

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/1398e615-4644-4ac5-8129-a8b9c88e6eb0.png)

### Count with filters

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/8fdf5cd6-549a-49ae-9060-7fd286cd760a.png)

## How did you test this code?

- Tested with different data types (persons, events, groups, sessions)
- Verified count accuracy with and without filters
- Confirmed loading states display correctly
- Checked that count queries don't impact main query performance
- Tested with large datasets to ensure counts are displayed efficiently

refactor(logs): replace logsLogic with logsSceneLogic across components (#45334)

## Problem

Product convention uses '<product>SceneLogic` as the naming convention for scene logic, which defines a logical barrier between component and scene logic. Currently our scene logic is tightly coupled to lots of logs components.

## Changes

Renamed `logsLogic` to `logsSceneLogic` to better reflect its role as a scene logic, improving code clarity and maintainability.

This is the first step on a path to reduce the responsibility of the logs scene logic.

feat(surveys): Improve guided survey wizard UX (#45185) ## Summary - Add inline question type selector with QuestionTypeChip dropdown - Add question-specific editing options (rating scale, choices, link URL) - Add "Submit on select" option for rating and choice questions - Add animated confirmation screen toggle with expand/collapse - Add cancel button to AddQuestionButton expanded state - Fix preview pagination to count confirmation screen properly - Default to Clean theme for better compatibility - Rename Link question type to "Link / Announcement" - Disable "Submit on select" when open-ended choice is present ## Test plan - Create a new survey using the wizard - Verify question type can be changed inline via dropdown chip - Verify rating questions show display type, scale, and bound labels - Verify choice questions show choices editor with add/remove - Verify "Submit on select" checkbox works and is disabled with open-ended choices - Verify confirmation screen toggle animates smoothly - Verify preview pagination shows correct counts (e.g., 2/3 with confirmation)

fix: Fix incorrect assumptions about `uiHost`/`apiHost` on toolbar (#44853)

We made a big mess with `uiHost` and `apiHost` in the toolbar. We're only getting the UI host in the initialization script (look at posthog-js) but we're using it both to redirect to posthog (should be UI Host) and also download CSS (should be API host to match JS assets).

To fix that, rather than looking at the prop we're passing in we can be much smarter and just use the `posthog` instance instead and use the more accurate between `uiHost`/`apiHost`. This means we don't use the prop anymore but we gotta keep it there for older `posthog-js` instances where we don't have access to `posthog`

fix: Insights data validation (#45206) ## Problem To enhance data integrity and access control, we're improving permission validation for how insights are associated with dashboards. This ensures that users can only modify dashboard-insight relationships (adding or removing) if they have the appropriate edit permissions for the target dashboard. ## Changes This PR introduces permission checks in the Insight API: - When creating an insight, a check is added to ensure the user has edit permissions on any specified dashboards before the insight is added to them. - When updating an insight, a check is added to ensure the user has edit permissions on dashboards from which the insight is being removed. - New tests have been added to `ee/api/test/test_insight.py` to cover these permission validation scenarios for both adding and removing insights from restricted dashboards. ## How did you test this code? New automated tests (`test_non_admin_user_cannot_remove_an_insight_from_a_restricted_dashboard` and `test_non_admin_user_cannot_create_insight_on_restricted_dashboard`) were added to `ee/api/test/test_insight.py` to verify the permission checks function correctly. ## Publish to changelog? no --- [Slack Thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0428JHABK2/p1768508262390949?thread_ts=1768508262.390949&cid=C0428JHABK2)

## Problem

We got feedback on a ticket: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/46912

that pointed out that its a bad experience when the event occurs outside of the scope of the recording in the session (during "load" for example)

For this filter rely on `all_urls` property under the hood to ensure its visited in the scope of the recording

fix(comments): extract mentions from rich text on backend (#45078)

## Problem

We got a ticket about missing mention emails. The robot helped me write scripts to confirm: 

  1. Email notification settings - Confirmed all relevant users have discussions_mentioned enabled
  2. Team access verification - Verified users have proper team membership
  3. MessagingRecord queries - Found zero discussions_user_mentioned email attempts
  5. Rich content inspection - Examined actual HeyGen comments and found mention data only in rich_content field


  Frontend sends mentions in rich_content JSON but the mentions field arrives empty at the API. Backend was checking if mentions: which was always false, so `send_discussions_mentioned.delay()` never executed.


<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes
Backend now extracts mention user IDs from rich_content.content[].attrs.id if the explicit mentions field is empty.

i'm not 100% sure why the attempt to extract on the front end isnt working for, but this is at least a fallback to check the rich_content again if the explicit metions array is empty...

feat(product tours): add display frequency optoins for announcements, and clarify for tours (#45174) ## Problem the default display frequency for _all_ tours right now (incl announcements & banners) is that we show until the tour is completed or dismissed. this is fine for tours, but makes no sense for banners or announcements! depends on https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/pull/2908 to actually work ## Changes this PR introduces the concept of "display frequency" with three options: 1. `always` -> always show, non-dismissible, etc 2. `until_interacted` -> show until user has some interaction with the tour (button click, dismiss, etc) 3. `once` -> only show once, regardless of whether the user interacted note: without careful UX, **_these options will be confusing_**. so, we only expose certain options and defaults per tour type: - **modal announcements**: - `show_once` (default) - `until_interacted` - **banners**: - `until_interacted` (default) - `always` - **tours**: have no options right now, and use `until_interacted` by default. - future: we'll probably add all the options, but need to clean up / think through resumeable tours. they are "resumable" now, but only in a given session. | announcement | banner | tour | | --- | --- | --- | | ![Screenshot 2026-01-15 at 9.50.02 AM.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/ebc0de93-f88c-49f3-a075-3f7ca6ebfeed.png)

feat(logs): add history tracking for filters (#45160)

## Problem

Users need a way to quickly access their recent filter configurations when working with logs. Currently, there's no way to recall previously used filter combinations without manually re-entering them.

## Changes

Added a filter history feature to the Logs interface that:

- Stores up to 10 recent filter configurations
- Displays them in a dropdown menu with relevant details
- Allows users to restore previous filter states with a single click
- Includes a "Clear history" option to reset the history

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/051d4490-eee3-49be-a07b-25ed78baec58.png)

## How did you test this code?

- Tested filter history persistence by applying various filter combinations and verifying they appear in the dropdown
- Verified that clicking on a history entry correctly restores all filter parameters
- Confirmed that duplicate filter entries aren't added to avoid clutter
- Tested the clear history functionality
- Verified that filter history is properly persisted between page reloads

## Add to changelog?

YES

fix(logs): prevent query from running if newly added filter has no value (#45141)

## Problem

When users are in the process of selecting a filter value in the logs interface, the query is being run prematurely before they've completed their selection. This creates unnecessary API calls and potentially confusing results.

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/faecfaba-3660-4eb7-b790-e94bc2d2f6d9.png)

## Changes

Added a check in the `setFilterGroup` function to prevent running a query when there's a filter without a value. The code now detects incomplete filters (where a key exists but the value is null or an empty array) and exits early in these cases.

This change ensures that queries only run after users have fully specified their filter criteria.

## How did you test this code?

Manually tested by:
1. Opening the logs interface
2. Adding a filter
3. Verifying that no query runs while selecting a filter value
4. Confirming that the query runs properly once a value is selected

## Publish to changelog?

No

chore(customers): Polish customer profiles (#44827)

## Problem

There are a few UX problems with customer profile views:
- People can hit backspace and delete the whole content
- Whenever they click the three-dot menu, the actions that are there do not really reflect the behavior that's intended for the profile canvases

Let's change that by making the canvases really non-editable whenever they are set to be non-editable and also add a way to customize which actions should be displayed in each notebook node. 

## Changes

- Added custom menu items support for notebook nodes
- Enabled settings button visibility in canvas mode even when not in edit mode
- Implemented custom menu items for profile-specific nodes (PersonFeed, Issues, LLMTrace, UsageMetrics, ZendeskTickets)
- Added ability to remove nodes directly from the customer profile
- Fixed isEditable logic to properly handle canvas mode

https://www.loom.com/share/a8646334818e449f85491e2c05932a21
## How did you test this code?

I tested this by:
- Verifying that settings buttons appear in canvas mode
- Confirming that custom menu items work correctly for each node type
- Testing the remove functionality for each node type in the customer profile
- Ensuring that the default menu items still work correctly for other node types

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->
The endpointSceneLogic was connecting to endpointLogic without passing the tabId prop, causing it to connect to an unkeyed default instance instead of the properly keyed instance. Additionally, the URL handler was always calling loadEndpoint() even when just switching tabs.

## Changes

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->
<!-- If a reference design was involved, include a link to the relevant Figma frame! -->

Changes:
- Add explicit props() declaration with EndpointSceneLogicProps
- Pass tabId to endpointLogic in connect() for proper keying
- Add endpointName state to track current endpoint
- Add early exit check in URL handler to skip reload when viewing the same endpoint (only tab changed)

PostHog AI needs a system to display service alerts to users when there are issues with the AI service, alongside the existing changelog functionality. Bonus: the Christmas messages have been removed.

feat(logs): reduce layout shift by keeping query button text static (#44933)

## Problem

The current refresh button causes layout shift when switching between 'search' and 'loading', additionally, this button is closer to 'refresh' functionality than search.

## Changes

Updated the button text in the LogsFilters component to consistently show "Refresh" instead of "Loading..." when logs are loading but live tail is not running. This provides clearer user feedback about the action the button will perform.

Before, the button text would change between three states:

- "Tailing..." when live tail is running
- "Loading..." when logs are loading
- "Search" when idle

Now, the button text is:

- "Tailing..." when live tail is running
- "Refresh" in all other states

## How did you test this code?

Tested the component in various states:

- When logs are loading
- When live tail is running
- When the component is in idle state

Verified that the button text displays correctly in each scenario and that the functionality remains unchanged.

## Publish to changelog?

No

The trace clustering labeling agent fails in production with "Anthropic API key is not configured" error. OpenAI API keys are already configured in the production environment.

Cost comparison:
| Model | Input (per MTok) | Output (per MTok) |
|-------|------------------|-------------------|
| Claude Sonnet 4.5 (old) | $3.00 | $15.00 |
| GPT-5.1 (new) | $1.25 | $10.00 |

- Verified imports and linting with ruff

## Problem
Refactoring the chat agent architecture to improve code organization and reusability by moving common agent functionality to the core module. This is needed for the plan mode and research agent.

- Split prompt building functionality into separate files for better organization
- Created dedicated toolkit and prompt builder classes for the chat agent
- Removed unused notebook-related code and feature flags

we accept external data and pass it to filtering

the robot says

```python
def safely_get_queryset(self, queryset):
    return queryset.filter(**self.request.GET.dict())
```
**Attack Example:**
```
GET /api/roles/?id__gte=1&name__regex=^.*
GET /api/roles/?created_at__year=2024&id__in=1,2,3
GET /api/roles/?organization__name__icontains=secret
```
we aren't using this filtering from the UI - i don't _think_
so let's just remove it?
could there be API clients using it?

## Problem

Project deletion fails when batch exports were previously deleted:
https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/46442

Error: `workflow not found for ID: temporal-sys-scheduler:0196cdba-59c1-0001-3ef7-355ef9a63b86`

## Changes
- Filter out soft-deleted batch exports (`deleted=False`)
- Handle `BatchExportServiceScheduleNotFound` exception for edge cases

> NOTE: The fix handles the case where batch export Temporal schedules are missing, but I'm not entirely clear on the root cause. There may be an issue with cascade deletion for batch exports, or somehow we're ending up with orphaned records without Temporal schedules.

feat(logs): Add log selection tracking and clear selection functionality (#44708)

## Problem

We need to track user interactions with log selection features to better understand how users are interacting with the logs viewer.

## Changes

Added PostHog event tracking for the following log selection actions:
- Added tracking when users select or unselect individual logs
- Added tracking when users clear their log selection, including the count of logs that were selected
- Added tracking when users select a range of logs, including the count of logs selected
- Added tracking when users select all logs, including the count of logs selected

## How did you test this code?

Manually tested each selection action in the logs viewer and verified that the appropriate PostHog events were captured with the correct properties.

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(logs): add copy log message and link buttons in LogDetailsModal (#44661)

## Problem

The log details modal lacks convenient options for users to copy log messages or share links to specific logs, making it harder to collaborate or reference logs in other contexts.

## Changes

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/8476f09e-d0d5-469a-93fd-82150480888a.png)

Enhanced the Log Details Modal with additional functionality:
- Added a copy button to easily copy the log message to clipboard
- Added a link button to copy a direct link to the specific log
- Replaced the default close button with a custom one in the header
- Improved the header layout with better organization of controls

## How did you test this code?

Manually tested the new buttons in the Log Details Modal:
- Verified the copy log message button correctly copies the log content to clipboard
- Confirmed the copy link button generates and copies a shareable link to the specific log
- Tested the close button functionality to ensure it properly dismisses the modal
- Checked that the UI layout remains responsive and visually consistent

feat(ph-ai): generic artifact manager with content handlers (#44203)

## Problem
This PR introduces a new artifact handler system to improve how we manage different types of artifacts (visualizations, notebooks) in the AI assistant. The current implementation lacks a structured way to handle different artifact types, making it difficult to add new types or manage their lifecycle.

## Changes
- Created a new handler-based architecture for artifacts with a registry system
- Implemented handlers for visualization and notebook artifacts
- Refactored ArtifactManager to use the new handler system
- Added enrichment capabilities to resolve references in notebook artifacts
- Improved type safety with proper generics and type narrowing
- Updated tools to work with the new artifact system

## How did you test this code?
- Added comprehensive unit tests for the new handler classes
- Updated existing tests to work with the new system

feat(product tours): precompute step HTML content (#44373)

## Problem

with the new rich text editor, building HTML from tiptap content becomes more complex. it'd be foolish to extend and maintain the existing [`renderTipTapContent`](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/blob/bc7b7dd8973130bcaa8e407ec66a51eb6e444bf1/packages/browser/src/extensions/product-tours/product-tours-utils.ts#L161) function.

tiptap provides an extension for generating HTML, so our options are:

1. bundle this with the SDK and generate HTML on the fly
2. precompute HTML on tour save, and render that in the SDK (with html sanitization)

both options increase the bundle size, but the 2nd option is less, and keeps the sdk as lean as possible

feat(surveys): safer two-step delete flow for surveys (#44656)

## Problem

Accidental permanent deletion of surveys is risky. Previously:
- Delete button was always available, even for running surveys
- Archive was only available for stopped surveys
- No clear warning about what would be permanently lost

## Solution

Implements a safer two-step deletion process:

1. **Archive first** - Now available for any non-archived survey (draft, running, or stopped)
   - If archiving a running survey, it automatically stops the survey first
   - Shows a clear warning when archiving a running survey
   - Button text changes to "Stop and archive" for running surveys

2. **Delete only after archiving** - Permanent deletion now requires archiving first
   - Delete button only appears for already-archived surveys
   - Enhanced warning dialog: "Permanently delete this survey?"
   - Clarifies that survey response events in the data are preserved
   - Button renamed to "Delete permanently" for clarity

## Changes

- `surveyLogic.tsx`: Updated `archiveSurvey` to stop running surveys before archiving
- `SurveysTable.tsx`: Archive available for all non-archived surveys, delete only for archived
- `SurveyView.tsx`: Delete button only shows for archived surveys

## How did you test this code?

- All 197 survey tests pass
- Manual verification of archive/delete button visibility based on survey state

fix: we don't need to hit clickhouse because a series was renamed (#44443)

I had a 180 day series open  
And renamed it  
We reloaded the data  
It took ages

Frustration swelled in the heart of the american bear #muppetsMovie

It occurs to me that there's no reason for us to load data when we rename a series

---

turns out we already ignored series names in the cache key
so when loading a query after a rename we would return the cached value

but that's wrong because the cached value includes the original series name

so at some point someone had changed the front end to force the query on a refresh

---

at first i added the cache management to the front end, but now cache invalidation lived in two places

cache invalidation is always the hardest bugs so we should not multiply front and backend together

---

instead the front end just makes the query request without forcing it

the backend checks if it needs to patch the series names,
updates the cached data,
and returns the cached data,

that way we skip the CH query but the cache is always correct for the data

---

## follow-ups

we can extend this approach (with slightly more difficulty)

- support more than trends queries for this skipping
- deleted series
- duplicated series
- reducing date range

feat(logs): click log row to open details modal (#44614)

## Problem

Opening log details required hovering to reveal the FAB, then clicking the "View details" button. This felt clunky compared to the intuitive pattern of clicking a row to see details.

## Changes

![2026-01-09 10.05.26.gif](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/a5b666fb-13fd-40b0-98ea-d5617aed0354.gif)

- **Click to open details**: Clicking a log row now opens the details modal directly
- **Cursor remains independent**: Keyboard navigation (j/k) moves the cursor without opening the modal - Enter opens modal for the cursor position
- **Text selection preserved**: Checks `window.getSelection()` to avoid triggering modal when user is selecting text
- **Transparent modal overlay**: Allows clicking other log rows while modal is open to switch between logs
- **Proper event propagation**: FAB buttons and expand chevron don't bubble clicks to the row handler
- **Modal Overlay**: Also makes the modal overlay transparent as an affordance, to show that the user can still interact with the other logs.

## How did you test this code?

- Clicked log rows to verify modal opens
- Selected text to verify modal doesn't open during selection
- Used j/k navigation to verify cursor moves without opening modal
- Pressed Enter to verify modal opens for cursor position
- Clicked other rows while modal open to verify it switches logs
- Shift+clicked to verify range selection still works
- Clicked FAB buttons to verify they don't trigger row click

fix(logs-events): fix log details opened event not firing (#44609)

## Problem

The `openLogDetails` listener was checking `!values.isOpen` before capturing the analytics event, but in Kea, listeners run *after* reducers. By the time the listener executed, `isOpen` was already `true`, so the event never fired.

## Changes

- Replaced the listener with a `kea-subscriptions` subscription on `isOpen`
- Subscription receives `(newValue, previousValue)`, allowing proper detection of `false → true` transitions
- Analytics event now correctly fires when the modal opens

## How did you test this code?

Verified the analytics event fires on modal open and does not fire on subsequent actions while modal remains open.

No

fix(logs): handle hasLogs API failures gracefully (#44526)

## Problem

When checking if a team has logs, we're making unnecessary API calls and not handling failures gracefully. This impacts the user experience when the logs API is unavailable or slow.

## Changes

- Added a `retryWithBackoff` utility function to handle transient failures with exponential backoff
- Implemented caching for the `has_logs` endpoint to reduce unnecessary API calls
  - Cache positive results for 7 days (once you have logs, you always have logs)
- Improved error handling in the logs ingestion logic
  - Added a `teamHasLogsCheckFailed` state to track API failures
  - Show the logs viewer even when the has_logs check fails
- Added a helpful banner with setup guide link when logs setup check fails

## How did you test this code?

- Tested the retry utility with various error scenarios to ensure proper backoff behavior
- Verified caching works by checking network requests after initial load
- Simulated API failures to confirm the UI gracefully handles errors and shows the appropriate guidance
- Tested the full user flow to ensure a smooth experience in both success and failure cases

No

Follow-up to https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44478. All relevant info in there. This is PR 2/2, removing the legacy code paths.

refactor(cohorts): use query for duplicating existing cohorts (#44478)

## Problem

We are calling `insert_cohort_from_insight_filter` when duplicating a cohort. This in turn calls `insert_cohort_actors_into_ch`, which contains [legacy insight calculations code](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/api/cohort.py#L1467-L1502). This method is not used anywhere besides the cohort duplication any more. 

## Changes

We want to get rid of the legacy insight calculations code. I could just have removed the now unused code path related to that, but decided to go a step further and replace the cohort calculation with the modern query-based variant.

This is PR 1/2 where I replace the frontend side call with the query-based approach. PR 2/2 removes the legacy code: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44481

## How did you test this code?

Duplicated a static and a dynamic cohort locally. Adapted tests in the follow up PR.

feat(logs): add event tracking for activation metric discovery (#44419)

## Problem

We need to discover the activation metric for Logs. Per the [PostHog activation metrics guide](https://posthog.com/product-engineers/activation-metrics), this requires:

1. Tracking candidate events that might "hook" users
2. Waiting 3-6 months for data
3. Analyzing which event combinations correlate with 3-month retention
4. Implementing `has_activated_logs()` with the winning criteria

Currently, Logs has no event tracking - we can't discover the activation metric without data.

## Changes

**Event tracking** (`posthog.capture`) for candidate activation signals:

```
// logsLogic.tsx - query/filter layer
posthog.capture('logs query executed', { has_search_term, has_filters, severity_count, service_count })
posthog.capture('logs results returned', { count })
posthog.capture('logs no results returned')
posthog.capture('logs filter changed', { filter_type: 'search' | 'attributes' | 'severity' | 'service' | 'date_range', ... })
posthog.capture('logs live tail started')
posthog.capture('logs load more requested')

// logsViewerLogic.ts - viewer layer
posthog.capture('logs log expanded')
posthog.capture('logs log pinned')
posthog.capture('logs link copied')
posthog.capture('logs bulk copy', { count })
posthog.capture('logs exported', { format, count })
posthog.capture('logs column added', { attribute_key })

// logDetailsModalLogic.ts - details modal
posthog.capture('logs details opened')
posthog.capture('logs details tab changed', { tab })

// logExploreAILogic.ts
posthog.capture('logs ai explanation requested')
```

Filter events use `hasRunQuery` guard to avoid tracking on initial page load from URL sync. Clearing filters is also tracked (e.g., `service_count: 0`).

**Product intent contexts** - we now have 2:

- `LOGS_DOCS_VIEWED` (existing) - onboarding interest
- `LOGS_SET_FILTERS` (new) - fired on all filter-related actions so `contexts.get("logs_set_filters")` gives total filter interaction count for activation checking

**Kea refactor** - extracted URL sync into dedicated actions (`syncUrlAndRunQuery`, `syncUrlWithPageSize`, `syncUrlWithHighlight`) to avoid a conflict where having both a `listener` and `tabAwareActionToUrl` handler for the same action (e.g., `setDateRange`) prevented the URL from updating. Filter actions now trigger listeners which call the sync actions.

## How did you test this code?

- Verified events fire correctly by checking browser console/network
- Confirmed URL sync works after refactor
- Ran kea typegen to confirm no type errors
- Schema regenerated successfully

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat: Add new AI-based usecase onboarding (#44212)

This PRs does a handful of things to our onboarding:

- Looks at what docs pages we've accessed on the website and uses that as the default selection of products when people choose "Pick myself"
- Made the list of usecases more pleasant to the eye by using three columns in wider screens
- Simplified the `urls` object by having all go through a single `urls.onboarding()` helper

And then, more importantly, hidden by a feature flag, we have a new AI-based use case onboarding where we ask people what they expect from PostHog and we then suggest products based on that.
We'll run this change behind an experiment, of course, since people might not like having an AI there vs. just picking a use case themselves.

fix(replay): try to fix css issue in iframe (gated) (#43049)

## Problem

We now have 2 tickets that "look" like crossOrigin CSS issues.... but both arent quite that

Ticket 1: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/41248
Ticket 2: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/44080

Theory: 
 1. When the player first mounts, and begins playing "messed up CSS" version, the iframe document is still readyState: "loading" (confirmed with console logs)
  2. rrweb hasn't finished processing CSS data yet, so it creates external <link rel="stylesheet"> tags as fallbacks
  3. These external links fail to load due to CSP restrictions in the sandboxed iframe (sandbox="allow-same-origin")
  4. On remount (after Inspect DOM), the iframe is fully loaded, so rrweb creates inline <style> tags instead, which work

  Evidence:
  - Initial load: 47 <style> tags + 11 <link> tags (broken)
  - After remount (open/close "inspect dom"): 58 <style> tags + 0 <link> tags (working)


## Changes

 Wait for the iframe document to finish loading (DOMContentLoaded event) before setting up the player. This ensures rrweb processes all CSS as inline <style> tags on
  first mount.

  Gated behind feature flag REPLAY_WAIT_FOR_IFRAME_READY for safe rollout.
## How did you test this code?

have to test in prod because the issue does not repro locally (json downloaded), hence flag

feat(Sparkline): add tooltipRowCutoff prop to limit series in tooltip (#44321)

## Problem

The Sparkline component currently doesn't allow customization of the number of series shown in the tooltip, which means that we can't show more than 8 series - which is needed for the logs sparkline.

## Changes

Added a new optional prop `tooltipRowCutoff` to the Sparkline component that allows limiting the number of series displayed in the tooltip. The default value is set to 8 rows.

- Added TypeScript prop definition with JSDoc comment
- Added the prop to the component's parameter list
- Passed the value to the tooltip component via the `rowCutoff` prop

Tested by rendering Sparkline components with various numbers of data series and verifying that:
- The tooltip correctly limits displayed rows to the specified cutoff value
- The default behavior (8 rows) works as expected when no value is provided
- The component continues to function properly with all other existing features

feat(zen-mode): persist zen mode (#44315)

## Problem

The Zen Mode setting in the navigation logic is not persisted between sessions, causing users to lose their preferred view mode when refreshing or returning to the app.

## Changes

Added persistence to the Zen Mode state in the navigation logic by adding the `{ persist: true }` option to the `zenMode` reducer. This ensures that a user's preference for Zen Mode is remembered across browser sessions.

## How did you test this code?

1. Enabled Zen Mode in the application
2. Refreshed the page and verified that Zen Mode remained enabled
3. Disabled Zen Mode
4. Closed the browser and reopened the application
5. Verified that Zen Mode remained disabled as expected

## Publish to changelog?

No

feat(logs): add AI-powered log explanation endpoint (#44191)

## Problem

Engineers need better tools to quickly understand and troubleshoot log entries, especially during critical incidents. Currently, they have to manually analyze logs without AI assistance.

## Changes

Added a new AI-powered log explanation feature that:

- Creates a new API endpoint at `/api/environments/:id/logs/explainLogWithAI/` for generating AI explanations of log entries
- Implements a structured response format with:
    - Quick summary and severity assessment
    - Probable causes with confidence levels
    - Prioritized immediate actions
    - Technical explanation and key fields to investigate
- Uses OpenAI's GPT-4.1 model with carefully crafted prompts designed for 2AM debugging scenarios
- Includes caching to improve performance and reduce API costs
- Applies rate limiting to prevent abuse
- Requires organization approval for AI data processing

## How did you test this code?

- Added comprehensive unit tests for all components:
    - Template loading and rendering
    - OpenAI API integration with proper error handling
    - Log fetching from ClickHouse
    - API endpoint functionality including authentication, permissions, and caching
- Manually tested with sample log entries to verify the quality of explanations

feat(LogRowFAB): add floating action button group for log actions (#44134)

### Problem

The current log viewer makes common row-level actions (e.g. prettifying JSON, pinning logs, copying messages/links) hard to access and difficult to scale as log content becomes wider and more interactive.

Actions are tightly coupled to table layout, which complicates both UI evolution and horizontal navigation.

---

### Changes

> This PR improves structure and ownership around row actions and scrolling; it does **not** aim to settle the final horizontal scroll model.



**UI / Structure**
![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/beed34fd-8e5d-435a-966f-3389d2d57ce3.png)
- Introduced a new `LogRowFAB` component to own row-level actions
- Replaced the fixed-width actions column with a floating action button (FAB) that overlays the row
- Added a `FABGroup` component to visually group related actions
- Ensured the FAB appears only on hover for a cleaner default state

**Actions**

- Added FAB buttons for:
    - Prettifying JSON
    - Pinning logs
    - Copying log messages
    - Copying direct links to logs
- Added horizontal scroll controls for navigating wide log content

**Scrolling / Ownership**

- Split the existing cell scroll logic into two focused hooks:
    - `useCellScrollRef` — owns the scrollable element
    - `useCellScrollControls` — owns scroll interactions
- This improves separation of concerns and makes future scroll model changes easier

This PR is safe to land independently and preserves existing behavior, while setting up clearer ownership for future iteration.

---

### How did you test this code?

- Verified all FAB actions work correctly (prettify, pin, copy, link)
- Tested horizontal scrolling via both button controls and direct interaction
- Confirmed correct FAB positioning and visibility on hover
- Tested with wrapped and unwrapped log messages
- Checked behavior across different screen sizes and log content shapes

### Changelog

fix: improve hobby deployment diagnostics and container stability (#44208)

# Improved container startup and error diagnostics for Hobby deployments

This PR enhances the reliability and debuggability of Hobby deployments by:

1. Adding a `kafka-init` service that creates required Kafka topics before dependent services start
2. Improving error logging in worker scripts with better exit trapping and diagnostics
3. Enhancing container health checks and failure detection in the deployment process
4. Adding more comprehensive error logging when containers fail to start
5. Showing container logs during failed stack starts to aid troubleshooting
6. Improving service dependency ordering to ensure proper startup sequence

These changes help prevent issues where services like `cymbal` would fail on startup due to missing Kafka topics, and make it easier to diagnose problems when they occur.

we have higher concurrency limits. we now need to raise rate limits on materialized endpoint execution.

## Changes

- Add rate_limit.py with caching helpers and throttle classes
- Cache materialization status in Redis (1hr TTL with write-through)
- Set cache on Temporal workflow completion (success/failure)

- Lazy load cache on first request for existing materializations

- Materialized: 1200/min burst, 12000/hr sustained (5x higher)

feat: Expand shortcuts around the app (#43951)

Let's make use of our new shortcuts bar and add some actual shortcuts to it!

Most of them are very simple but they are extremely extensible. Adding these here means that teams know how to implement shortcuts in the future since they can always refer back to the existing shortcuts. I tried adding it to the biggest number of products possible. Some products already have their own shortcuts - like Logs - so I didn't add many global ones for them since they're handling shortcuts themselves.

There's a new hook to be able to register shortcuts even when there isn't a button to be clicked - like toggilng hedgehog mode on (which we lost the ability to do since the new shortcut bar, oh no)!

feat: allow customizing text colors on surveys (#43615)

## Problem

The survey appearance customization UI needs improvement to be more organized and user-friendly. The current layout doesn't group related options logically and lacks explicit text color controls.

Requires https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/pull/2787 to be in before this is ready to ship

## Changes

- Reorganized the survey appearance options into logical subgroups (Survey background, Inputs and ratings, Submit button)
- Added explicit text color controls with "auto-contrast" hints
- Improved responsive layout with better grid structure (1 column on mobile, 3 columns on larger screens)
- Updated the "inherit" font label to be more concise

Before:

![CleanShot 2025-12-17 at 19.07.19@2x.jpg](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/3efe948e-54bb-46c3-ada4-6a481f150001.jpg)

After:

![CleanShot 2025-12-17 at 19.06.36@2x.jpg](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/b072dabe-2984-4d57-812a-ed7c95b060d7.jpg)

## How did you test this code?

Tested the UI in both mobile and desktop viewports to ensure the responsive layout works correctly. Verified that all color controls function properly and that the auto-contrast hint displays correctly. Confirmed that the new text color fields are properly saved and applied to surveys.

## Changelog

feat(endpoints): add scheduled job to deactivate stale materializations (#43344)

# problem
might not be a problem yet tbf, but if someone materializes something and doesn't use it - we don't wanna keep charging them for it (and keep abusing our resources)

# solution

Adds a daily Celery task that checks for materialized endpoints that haven't been executed by users in over 30 days but are still being actively materialized. For these stale endpoints, the materialization is deactivated.

The task:
- Finds endpoints with active materializations (ran by API key in past 24h)
- Deactivates materializations for endpoints not executed in 30+ days

# Tests
- unit tests and tested locally

# Changelog

fix: Reapply "feat(product tours): create/edit in toolbar more easily" (#43723) (#43735)

## Problem

i added a new prop to the authorized domain logic to support product tours, it caused kea errors in prod due to missing props...

sample tickets:

- https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/45497
- https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/45487

the recordings on these show the error always happened after clicking "add authorized URL"

this button opens the `AuthorizedUrlForm`, which contains a kea form:

```typescript
<Form
  logic={authorizedUrlListLogic}
  props={{
    actionId: actionId ?? null,
    experimentId: experimentId ?? null,
    type,
    allowWildCards,
  }}
/>
```

...i missed adding `productTourId` here, and kea forms do not type-check the props 😥

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->

<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes

- re-applies the [original commit](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/43623)
- updates all usages of `authorizedUrlListLogic`, whether directly or via `Form`, to include all required props
- adds playwright tests to ensure this form works for toolbar and web analytics pages!!

refactor: Remove `inline-snapshot` library (#43740)

We currently have 2 different ways to take snapshots from code, let's settle in one. I believe our `unittest_snapshot` fixture is much more widely used - used a lot for Clickhouse - so I chose that one! I also find it better that the snapshots are kept outside the test and in a separate file instead since this makes reading the test case much easier.

This also means snapshots are regenerated in PRs simplifying our life a little

fix: sanitize Redis URL logging to prevent credential leakage and include port for disambiguation (#43474)

## Problem

PR #43472 added Redis URL logging to identify which instance is failing, but URLs may contain credentials in format `redis://:password@host:port`, leaking passwords in logs. Additionally, when multiple Redis connections share the same host but use different ports, the logs need to distinguish between them.

## Changes

Added `getRedisHost()` helper that extracts only the host portion from Redis URLs and includes the port for proper identification:

```typescript
function getRedisHost(url: string, options?: RedisOptions): string {
    try {
        const parsed = new URL(url)
        // Returns hostname:port, excluding credentials
        return parsed.host || '[sanitized-redis-host]'
    } catch {
        // Strips credentials from malformed strings and appends port from options
        const atIndex = url.lastIndexOf('@')
        const hostname = atIndex >= 0 ? url.substring(atIndex + 1) : url
        if (options?.port && !hostname.includes(':')) {
            return `${hostname}:${options.port}`
        }
        return hostname
    }
}
```

**Sanitization behavior:**
- `redis://:password@localhost:6379` → `localhost:6379` (credentials removed, port included)
- `redis://localhost:6379` → `localhost:6379` (unchanged)
- `posthog-redis` + `{ port: 6379 }` → `posthog-redis:6379` (port from options included)
- `posthog-redis` + `{ port: 6479 }` → `posthog-redis:6479` (different port distinguishable)
- `:password@localhost` + `{ port: 6379 }` → `localhost:6379` (malformed URLs sanitized, port added)
- URLs without host → `[sanitized-redis-host]` (safe placeholder)

Updated three logging statements in `createRedisClient()` to use sanitized host with port instead of full URL.

## How did you test this code?

Verified 15+ test cases covering:
- Valid URLs with/without credentials
- Plain hostnames with port from options
- Multiple connections with same host but different ports (e.g., `posthog-redis:6379` vs `posthog-redis:6479`)
- Malformed strings and edge cases
- Confirmed zero credential leakage in all scenarios

feat(customers): Add search and hide null properties to notebook node properties (#43589)

## Problem

The current properties display in notebooks for both person and group properties lacks filtering capabilities, making it difficult for users to find specific properties when dealing with large property sets.

## Changes

Enhanced the Properties component in notebooks with improved functionality:

- Added search functionality to filter properties by name or value
- Added option to show/hide null values
- Improved property sorting with pinned properties displayed first
- Maintained consistent sorting order within pinned and unpinned groups
- Added count indicators showing how many properties are displayed vs. total
- Simplified the component interface by passing the full properties object and pinned property list instead of pre-filtered objects

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/d2a91355-14f7-4c3c-9778-89829c020b58.png)![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/f64c2b48-50ab-4457-a82d-f2a749f59bf6.png)![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/c40bf7f4-1b3a-4333-b360-3e30d38ee9ef.png)

## How did you test this code?

Tested with various person and group property datasets, verifying:

- Search functionality correctly filters properties by both key and value
- Null value filtering works as expected
- Pinned properties consistently appear at the top of the list
- Property counts are accurate when filtering is applied
- Sorting behavior maintains expected order in all scenarios

## Problem

## Changes

Renamed the "Feed" tab to "Profile" in both the Persons and Groups scenes:

- Updated tab labels from "Feed" to "Profile" in the UI
- Changed the corresponding enum values in `PersonsTabType` and `GroupsTabType` from `FEED` to `PROFILE`
- Updated data attributes from `data-attr="persons-feed-tab"` to `data-attr="persons-profile-tab"` and `data-attr="groups-feed-tab"` to `data-attr="groups-profile-tab"`
- Modified the default tab selection logic to use "profile" instead of "feed"

## How did you test this code?

Tested by:
- Verifying the tab name changes appear correctly in the UI for both Persons and Groups pages
- Confirming that navigation between tabs continues to work as expected
- Ensuring the default tab selection logic correctly defaults to "profile" when customer analytics is enabled

## Problem

Customer Analytics moving to beta, and we need to collect user feedback to improve the product. Adding feedback banners across key pages will encourage users to share their thoughts and report issues.

## Changes

Added a new `FeedbackBanner` component that displays a consistent feedback request banner across multiple pages in the Customer Analytics product:

- Added the component to the Group profile page
- Added the component to the Groups list page
- Added the component to the Person profile page
- Added the component to the People list page
- Added the component to the Customer Analytics dashboard

Each banner includes a unique feedback button ID to help track where feedback is coming from.

## How did you test this code?

Manually verified the banner appears correctly on all pages where it was added, confirming the styling is consistent and the feedback button is clickable with the correct ID.

feat(ph-ai): notebook artifacts UI (#42696)

## Problem
New `NotebookArtifactAnswer` to support in-chat Notebooks, and conversion of those to TipTap notebooks.

## Changes
- Added new block types to the schema:
  - `LoadingBlock`: Displays loading state for artifacts being processed
  - `ErrorBlock`: Shows error messages when artifacts fail to load
- Updated `VisualizationBlock` to directly contain query data instead of referencing by ID
- Created a new `NotebookArtifactAnswer` component to render notebook artifacts in Max
- Added utility function `markdownToTiptap` to convert markdown to Tiptap format for notebook creation
- Added storybook examples for different notebook artifact scenarios
- Improved streaming message handling in `maxThreadLogic` with `finalizeStreamingMessages` action (rationale: with notebooks, we might have more than one streaming message at once, so we need to clean failed streamed messages when restarting the conversation, adding a temp id, so they don't get mistakenly replaced by new streaming content)


## How did you test this code?
Stories + tests + locally (needs the next PR in the stack to run)

feat(replay): disable button if no recording exists (#42173)

## Problem

we show a CTA to watch recordings from error tracking, which often goes to a 404 page, perhaps we should just disable the button if no recording exists, seems like a slight less "is this a bug?" experience

generally it would be great if i could instead return a "missing_reason/missing_type" instead that corresponds to the reasons @veryayskiy  is building for missing recordings, so i can give a better tooltip, but i'll do that once that merges :) 

## Changes

- on backend, once we fetch all the errors, group all session ids and fetch recordings for those session ids
- add a property has_recording to the response
- check this on the front end when determining if disabled

feat(customers): Configure dashboard with AI (#43164)

## Problem

The Customer Analytics dashboard configuration lacks an AI-assisted setup option, making it difficult for users to properly configure events for analytics tracking.

## Changes

Added an AI configuration button to each event selector in the Customer Analytics dashboard configuration. This allows users to get AI-assisted recommendations for configuring:
- Activity events
- Signup pageview events
- Signup events
- Subscription events
- Payment events

fix: ensure negative property things are across the whole session for replay fitlers (#43399)

when we're querying a negative thing in replay

humans mean "across the whole session"

if our query looks per event then we end up with

session 1 - event 1 - no email property
session 1 - event 2 - email property

when the query is show sessions with no email

if we only look per event then we will match session 1

but humans never mean that

so when there is a negative query, we count the positive equivalent and check that the count is 0

fix(ph-ai): fix conversation autoscroll (#43379)

## Problem
PostHog AI's conversation thread doesn't autoscroll when in full screen mode. In sidebar mode, sometimes it scrolls but not to reach the bottom of the div.

## Changes
- Fixed `getScrollableContainer` in `maxLogic.tsx` to correctly identify the parent container.
- Updated `ThreadAutoScroller` to always scroll to bottom when the streaming ends
